reports.excerpt: A 28-page NERAI Probability Forecast by Serhan Ayik. Pakistan's authoritarianism index spike (level 0.012, 99.4th percentile, +438% 7-day, z90=3.677) signals not an imminent military coup but the quantitative signature of institutional codification — the 27th Constitutional Amendment, passed November 2025 and analysed by Chatham House on 2 December 2025, formally embeds military influence through judicial subordination and Judicial Commission restructuring. Equilibrium-phase consolidation analogous to Turkey post-2016 or Hungary post-2010, not rupture-phase instability. Three forward scenarios: Managed Hybrid Equilibrium 60% (base), Escalation to Overt Military Rule 25% (stress), Negotiated Liberalisation 15% (tail). 14-day watch list anchored to IMF EFF Fourth Review (Q3 2026), Supreme Court PTI rulings, SBP usable-reserves thresholds ($10bn floor / $15bn comfort), Khan trial trajectory, and Corps Commanders Conference rhetoric. Counter-thesis (opposition rhetoric noise hypothesis) fully engaged and rebutted. Probabilistic framing only — not a deterministic prediction.
reports.title: Codification, Not Coup: A NERAI Probability Forecast for Pakistan's Hybrid Authoritarian Equilibrium Through Mid-2027

reports.excerpt: A 22-page research brief by Serhan Ayik layering NERAI political-event signals on Sub-Saharan Africa's debt-distress denominator. Schotte's Credendo Risk Insight set the structural parameters — debt-service ratios, bilateral-creditor composition, the China–Paris Club coordination deficit. This brief is the numerator: the NERAI signals that determine when and how debt vulnerability converts into distress. Ethiopia at the apex of a support-withdrawal cycle (Protest 100/100 ceiling, IFS forecast −61%); Kenya hostage to IMF programme friction (International Support −56%); Ghana's post-restructuring fragility rising. Three transmission channels, one synchronised −56%/−60%/−64% collapse in 12-month donor support. Base case 55% Managed Muddling, 30% downside, 15% upside. 14-day watch list with monitoring thresholds for trade-credit insurers and Paris Club coordinators.
reports.title: Beyond the Debt Numbers: A Political & Event-Signal Layer on Sub-Saharan Africa's Debt-Distress Triggers (Q2 2026)

reports.excerpt: A 24-page probability forecast by Serhan Ayik. NERAI's Russia Coup Index at 53.5/100 (95th percentile, 90-day rising) with the 12-month forecast declining -36% (0.110 → 0.070) — the diagnostic signature of managed securitisation, not regime decay. Securitisation-as-Stabilisation framework: heightened threat perception channeled into visible institutional response (purges, FSO expansion, military resource reallocation) reinforces elite coordination rather than triggering cascading defection. Three forward scenarios (Managed Securitisation 35%, Constrained Escalation 45%, Rapid Consolidation 20%) with full counter-thesis (Kendall-Taylor / Snegovaya cascade interpretation) and rebuttal. Probabilistic framing only — not a deterministic prediction.
reports.title: The Securitization Equilibrium: A NERAI Probability Forecast for Russian Regime Stability and Coup Risk Through May 2027

reports.excerpt: Practitioner snapshot by Serhan Ayik. Eight markets where Belgian and Dutch SME exporters carry concentrated risk exposure in Q2 2026: Türkiye (dissent at 99th percentile, IMF Article IV in June), Egypt (human rights at maximal alert, fiscal stress), Algeria, Tunisia, Nigeria (instability ~90th pct, FX-reserve squeeze), Morocco (most stable in set), Bangladesh, Indonesia. Dominant cross-market theme: synchronised forecast decline in International Support across the entire panel through 2027 — a structural liquidity squeeze. Includes 60-day watch list, NERAI data integration, and a 60-second CFO summary table for broker-channel conversations.
reports.title: Belgium-Netherlands SME Export Risk Snapshot — Q2 2026

reports.excerpt: Senior Fellow research brief by Serhan Ayik. NERAI's 15 May 2026 signal cluster places ethnic-religious violence (z=4.014), political repression (z=3.848), institutional deterioration (z=3.787) and government instability (z=3.222) simultaneously at the 98th-99.4th percentile — a pre-electoral squeeze signature matching Pattern P5 leadership-transition dynamics. Three forward scenarios for the late-2026 vote: Peaceful Incumbent Retention 65% (base), Contested Incumbent Retention 25% (stress), Opposition Breakthrough or Disruption 10% (tail). Senegal's PASTEF government under President Faye is the critical external arbiter. 10-point 60-day watch list, eight policy tracks across investors, donors, ECOWAS, Senegal and US/UK.
reports.title: The Gambia 2026 — Pre-Election Strategic Risk Assessment

reports.excerpt: Senior Fellow research brief by Serhan Ayik. EU 95%+ dependent on TSMC/Samsung for sub-7nm logic through 2028. NERAI US-China Tension Index at 25.7 (96th percentile). Three forward scenarios: Slow Bifurcation 68% (base), Taiwan Quarantine 22%, Bifurcation Collapse 10%. Intel Magdeburg PAUSED; TSMC Dresden (ESMC) at 35% nameplate; STMicro-GlobalFoundries Crolles-2 in legacy-node ramp.
reports.title: EU Semiconductor Supply Chain — Geopolitical Risk Briefing 2026
US-China Strategic Realignment at the Precipice: Trump's Beijing Visit Amid Iran Escalation
reports.summary: Senior Fellow assessment by Kagan Serhan Ayik. NERAI bilateral Tension Index at 27.4 (97th percentile, 12-month range), Cooperation Index at 54.2. Base case (65%): 90-180 day tariff pause on $50-80B Chinese goods, $20-30B agricultural purchase target — mirroring 2018 Buenos Aires precedent that lasted 88 days. Contrarian thesis: Trump's domestic fragility (Political Instability 55.5 RISING) is a negotiating LIABILITY, not asset; Beijing reads weakness as license to demand early concessions. Tactical pause expires Q4 2026 as both powers' international support collapses 47–50%.

reports.title: Sparta 2.0: The European Defence Authority and the Recalibration of Continental Security Architecture
reports.summary: NERAI Strategic Analysis deep dive on the EU's most ambitious independent defence initiative. Three-scenario forecast: base case 60% probability of partial implementation by 2031, with €150–200B capability gap assessment and NATO compatibility analysis.

reports.summary: No escalating signals crossed the 0.50 threshold this week, marking a significant deviation from typical volatility patterns. NERAI composite stability confirmed.
